CORRECTION_v2.issue: Phase 7 original compared physical scroll lines (DSS) vs biblical verses (WLC) — incommensurable units. H=0.887 measured calligraphy/column-width autocorrelation, not textual structure.
CORRECTION_v2.fix: Re-segmented DSS by biblical verses using ETCBC chapter/verse mapping (100% coverage). Fair comparison: same unit, same text, 1100 years apart.

ORIGINAL_FLAWED_COMPARISON.note: INVALIDATED — kept for audit trail only
ORIGINAL_FLAWED_COMPARISON.cause: 94.9% of apparent delta_H was segmentation artifact, 5.1% textual

metrics_a_ORIGINAL.note: DSS metrics computed on physical scroll lines — valid for studying calligraphy, NOT for textual comparison

reasoning: When compared using the same segmentation unit (biblical verses), DSS 1QIsa^a (H=0.681) and WLC Isaiah (H=0.672) are statistically indistinguishable (p=0.71). The long-range memory structure is a robust, time-invariant property of the Hebrew biblical text under controlled transmission. 1,100 years of Masoretic copying neither degraded nor amplified it. This strengthens H4: controlled transmission regimes not only generate long-range correlations — they preserve them across millennia.

implications.H5a_status: not_confirmed — DSS does NOT show higher H than WLC when measured correctly
implications.H5b_status: not_confirmed — WLC does NOT show higher H than DSS
implications.actual_finding: Temporal invariance of H under controlled transmission
implications.H4_strengthened: The stability of H across 1,100 years is direct evidence that controlled transmission preserves long-range structure. Free transmission (Homer, Herodotus) does not show this stability.
implications.decay_model_status: ELIMINATED — the 0.018 H/century rate was an artifact of the segmentation mismatch

word_level_analysis.note: 26.6% word-level variants over 1,100 years, yet H is preserved. The variants are predominantly orthographic (plene/defective spelling) and do not affect the long-range correlation structure.
